name: Embalse de Torre de Abraham from — circular via the Sendero de la Colada de Navalrincón
name_original: Embalse de Torre de Abraham desde — circular por el Sendero de la Colada de Navalrincón
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around El Robledo are characterized by rolling hills, dense forests, and riverine landscapes, offering diverse terrain for cyclists. The region, particularly within the Montes de Toledo, features green hills and clear rivers, providing a scenic backdrop for rides. Elevation gains are generally moderate, making the area accessible for various skill levels. The natural surroundings include evergreen oaks and Mediterranean mountains, typical of central Spain. Best mountain bike trails around El Robledo The most popular mountain bike trail is Embalse de Torre de Abraham from — circular via the Sendero de la Colada de Navalrincón, a 31.1 miles (50.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 33 minutes to complete. This route features a moderate difficulty with gentle ascents. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is El Robledo — circular desde Porzuna, a moderate 28.1 miles (45.3 km) path. This trail offers varied terrain through the local countryside. Local mountain bikers also love the Matalasbestias – Ermita de San Isidro (Picón) loop from Porzuna, a 26.9 miles (43.3 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 49 minutes. Mountain biking around El Robledo is defined by green hills, dense forests, and river basins, with options for different ability levels. The network includes paths suitable for both leisurely rides and more engaging challenges. The routes in El Robledo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 15 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore El Robledo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around El Robledo?
A: There are at least 5 mountain bike trails detailed for the El Robledo area, all rated as moderate difficulty. These routes offer diverse terrain through green hills, dense forests, and riverine landscapes.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain biking trails in El Robledo?
A: The mountain bike trails around El Robledo are generally rated as moderate difficulty. They feature rolling hills and gentle ascents, making them accessible for a range of skill levels while still offering engaging challenges.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in El Robledo?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1186781868/embalse-de-torre-de-abraham-from-circular-via-the-sendero-de-la-colada-de-navalrincon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Torre de Abraham from — circular via the Sendero de la Colada de Navalrincón&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 50 km long, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1186792689/el-robledo-circular-desde-porzuna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Robledo — circular desde Porzuna&lt;/a&gt;, covering approximately 45 km.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: The terrain around El Robledo is characterized by rolling hills, dense forests, and riverine landscapes. You'll encounter green hills, evergreen oaks, and Mediterranean mountains, typical of central Spain. The routes often feature varied surfaces, including unpaved and natural sections.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in El Robledo?
A: The mountain bike routes in El Robledo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Users often praise the scenic backdrops, diverse terrain, and the overall enjoyable experience of riding through the region's natural beauty.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, you can find interesting points of interest. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5857254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colada de Navalrincón Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight in itself. You might also pass through or near settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5647847" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Robledo&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5577922" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcoba de los Montes&lt;/a&gt;, and religious buildings such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5539492" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish Church of Porzuna&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is El Robledo suitable for family-friendly mountain biking?
A: While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, the moderate difficulty of the trails and the scenic, natural surroundings suggest that some sections could be suitable for families with older children or those comfortable with moderate distances. The region's green hills and clear rivers offer a pleasant environment for outdoor activities.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in El Robledo?
A: Given the region's climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking, with milder temperatures. Summer can be quite warm, especially in the Montes de Toledo area, while winter might bring cooler temperatures, though trails are generally accessible.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes offer substantial distances. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14660720" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de Alcolea – CM-4109 loop from Porzuna&lt;/a&gt; is over 52 km, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167461" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Isidro Hermitage (Porzuna) – Parish Church of Porzuna loop from Parada de Autobús de Las Rabinadas&lt;/a&gt; is approximately 51 km, providing extended riding experiences.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while mountain biking in El Robledo?
A: El Robledo, particularly within the Montes de Toledo, is known for its rich biodiversity. It's a critical area for protected species such as the black stork and the Iberian lynx. While sightings are rare, you might encounter various bird species and other local wildlife in the dense forests and riverine areas.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for mountain biking in El Robledo?
A: The provided information does not indicate specific permits are required for mountain biking on the general trails around El Robledo. However, it's always advisable to check local regulations, especially if riding within protected natural park areas, to ensure compliance.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking trails in El Robledo?
A: While specific parking locations are not detailed for each trail, routes often start from or pass through villages like Porzuna. It is generally possible to find parking in or near these settlements, which serve as common starting points for outdoor activities in the region.
tips: Nice route between towns on the bullaque river
Viewpoint of the Pantano de la Torre de Abraham, beautiful views of it.

It is a pleasant and flat path on raised walkways through the interior of a riparian forest next to the Bullaque river bed, surrounded by lush riparian vegetation and also vegetation typical of Mediterranean forests. It allows you to appreciate specimens of willow, ash, durillo, gall oak, etc, and enjoy the song of birds such as the nightingale or the wagtail. It has simple explanatory panels on the plants that are observed, and is accessible to people with reduced mobility.

waypoints.name: Wooden bridge over the Bullaque River, El Torno
